children.text: The Place de la Concorde is one of the major public squares in Paris, France. Measuring 8.64 hectares in area, it is the largest square in the French capital. It is located in the city's eighth arrondissement, at the eastern end of the Champs-Élysées.

content-desc: Map of Place de la Concorde

children.text:
A large square in the middle of the city which is surrounded by iconic monuments all around.
There is a big garden on one side and some superb Renaissance Architecture buildings all around which provides you a feel of Paris.
According to me, one must start exploring Paris from this place and then proceed to other places because it helps get into the groove.
There is a metro station which is very adjacent to this place so one can take a Metro and then visit all the other places around by foot.
Highly recommended of course.

Beautiful palace in the center of Paris with views of monuments and quite a few monuments on its own. The obelisk is quite large and worth getting up close and seeing. Lots of traffic so be careful if you are driving or walking.
Great open space to people watch, take in an overview of landmark sites, and take photographs. Passed through a few times in transit. Busy but mostly in a good way.

So grand and so beautiful! When you stand right in front of the fountain, you are surrounded 360 degrees by pure overwhelming beauty. You can see the Eiffel Tower and the arc de Triomphe and the Madeline temple, all from the same spot!
Ride the big ferris wheel for a view of the city, or just admire the buildings surrounding the square. The neighbouring park is also worth a visit, connects to the Louvre in the other end.
Excelent experience will come back many times superb & magnifique Paris is magnifique I love it.
Very beautiful and historical place. Well preserved, clean and tourism attraction.

A public square, with lots of beautiful architectural pieces. If your a lover of history and architecture this is a must. Photographers get yourself there and have a field day. Definitely a selfie spot.
Amazing open space in the centre of paris. Wonderful view of the arc de troimphe.
It's one of those places you have to visit in Paris, usually the start or finish of most rallies and a meeting place for friends.  Monument is beautiful.
